title: Slow-cooker butternut squash soup
subtitle: Squash, ginger, and cumin blended smooth
excerpt: Butternut squash, onion, and ginger slow-cooked in vegetable stock for six hours, then blended smooth with smoked paprika and cumin. The slow cooker concentrates the sweetness of the squash in a way that a quick stovetop version does not. Finish with a swirl of cream.
sourceNotes: Butternut squash soup became a British restaurant and home kitchen staple from the 1990s as butternut squash became widely available. The slow cooker version concentrates the flavour more than a quick stovetop version because the squash caramelises slightly against the hot sides of the pot over the long cook. A stick blender finishes the soup in the pot.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well without the cream. Add cream when reheating.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make ahead and refrigerate. Reheat and add cream just before serving.

content.text: Add less stock than you think you need. Butternut squash releases moisture as it cooks, and a soup that starts at the right consistency will end up too thin. Start with 700 ml and add more after blending if needed. It is easier to thin a soup than to thicken it.

Add the {{butternut-squash}}, {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, {{black-pepper}}, and {{vegetable-oil}} to the slow cooker. Pour over the {{stock-vegetable}} and stir. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the squash is completely soft. Blend until smooth using a stick blender directly in the pot. If the soup is too thick, add more stock or water and blend again. Taste and adjust seasoning. Swirl in {{double-cream}} to finish if using.

items.cause: Squash released more liquid than expected and then reduced over 6 hours
items.fix: Add hot stock or water and blend again. Adjust until the consistency pours easily from a ladle

items.symptom: Soup tastes bland despite the squash
items.fix: Season generously at the end — blended soups absorb salt differently. A squeeze of lemon juice also brightens a flat soup

items.cause: Fresh ginger is strong when concentrated over 6 hours
items.fix: Start with 15 g rather than 20 g next time. A large pinch of sugar also balances excessive ginger heat

content.text: Butternut squash soup is the most reliable slow-cooker soup. The squash is forgiving, sweet, and produces a naturally smooth result when blended. It works as a weekday lunch or a dinner party starter. Serve with crusty bread and a swirl of cream or a scatter of toasted pumpkin seeds.
